\paragraph{Results summary.}
Across 220 simulation steps (example\\_water\\_nacl\\_davies), the coarse-grained free energy evolved from -157.4 to -346.2 (minimum -346.7). The electrolyte layer reports ionic strength $I=0.09963$~mol\,L$^{-1}$ and Debye length $\lambda_D=0.964$~nm. Spectral indicators yield a Dirac gap of order 1.145 (simulator units). Braid complexity (reduced word length) reaches 215. These metrics are qualitative graph-level proxies; quantitative agreement with experiment is not implied.
